summary: DHH highlights a new blog called 'On The Rails' by a London-based developer documenting their experience building a large-scale production web application with Ruby on Rails. He notes the blog's promising daily posting frequency and calls out a recent post about implementing authentication using form helpers. This is a brief community-building post from Rails' early days, promoting grassroots adoption.
key_points: A new blog is tracking the real-world learning curve of building a production Rails application
The blog's consistent daily posting schedule signals genuine engagement with the framework
Authentication implementation using form helpers is highlighted as a practical topic worth sharing
money_quotes: a new and very nice weblog tracking the learning curve of one London-based Railer
key_insight: In Rails' earliest days, DHH actively promoted individual developers documenting their learning journeys as a way to build community and demonstrate the framework's real-world viability.
